title: Boston EMS Superintendent-in-Chief dies after 40 years with the department
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Gill, 61, of West Roxbury, passed away after 40 years of service with Boston EMS.
Gill was promoted through ranks, serving as Paramedic, Deputy Superintendent, and Superintendent-in-Chief since 2020.
His contributions include founding the Boston EMS Honor Guard and serving as Chief Steward of the EMS union.

title: 5 Bruins prospects who could push for a spot in Boston’s lineup next season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Michael DiPietro could secure a Bruins' roster spot next season, being a cost-effective option with strong AHL performance.
Frederic Brunet is a candidate for Boston's defensive lineup, known for his offensive skills and potential pairing with Charlie McAvoy.
Matt Poitras, after setbacks, aims for a fourth-line center role, potentially adding speed and scoring to Boston's roster.

title: Lawrence man charged with murder after woman dies from serious head injury
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Tyler Halley, 21, is charged with murder after a woman's death from a severe head wound in Lawrence.
The incident occurred early Saturday at 240 Canal St., with the victim pronounced dead shortly after hospital arrival.
Lawrence and State Police, along with the Medical Examiner's Office, are investigating the death and Halley's involvement.

title: ‘Purest values of service’: Communities pay tribute after veteran Boston firefighter, Marine veteran killed battling Dorchester blaze
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Robert Kilduff Jr., a Boston firefighter and Marine veteran, died after falling from a third-story window during a Dorchester fire.
Kilduff served with the Boston Fire Department for 24 years and was a member of Rescue Company 2 and Local 718 union.
His wake is scheduled for May 31 and funeral on June 1; Tunnel to Towers Foundation will pay off his home's mortgage.

title: Jaylen Brown challenges Stephen A. Smith to debate at site like Harvard or MIT
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown challenges Stephen A. Smith to a live debate at Harvard or MIT in front of a live-streamed audience.
Brown criticizes the media industry, accusing Stephen A. Smith of prioritizing clickbait over journalistic integrity.
The feud began after Boston's playoff loss, with Brown dismissing Smith's criticisms as weak narratives without basis.

title: NBA Insider: Celtics, Heat are 2 teams ‘believed to intrigue’ Giannis in a blockbuster trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics and Heat are potential trade destinations for Giannis Antetokounmpo, according to NBA insider Marc Stein.
Boston would likely need a three-team trade involving Jaylen Brown to acquire Giannis from the Bucks.
The Hawks, Rockets, and Trail Blazers have shown interest in Jaylen Brown as part of potential trade deals.

title: Read what Bruce Springsteen had to say about Trump, ICE, and America in Boston Sunday
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bruce Springsteen criticized President Trump during his concert at TD Garden in Boston, calling the administration treasonous.
Springsteen spoke about ICE's actions in Minneapolis, highlighting the deaths of civilians Renee Good and Alex Pretti.
He urged the audience to actively defend American ideals, emphasizing themes of unity, democracy, and peaceful resistance.

title: Review and setlist: Springsteen eviscerates Trump at fiery TD Garden stop
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bruce Springsteen's TD Garden concert emphasized defending American ideals amid current political challenges.
The concert featured 27 songs, including political anthems and new songs addressing recent events.
Special guest Tom Morello joined Springsteen, delivering powerful performances and political messages.

title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on May 22, 2026, as part of their spring season.
Keith Lockhart conducted the Boston Pops, featuring American classics by Dudley Buck, Aaron Copland, and Richard Rodgers.
Odom Jr.'s set included 'Fly Me To The Moon' and 'Wait For It' from Hamilton, showcasing his Broadway and jazz influences.

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, of Chelsea faces charges including assault and battery on children under 14, and strangulation.
Incidents involved burning children with a lighter and a heated fork, and alleged violent assaults caught on video.
Bautista-Zepeda was located in Virginia and held without bail pending a dangerousness hearing on May 28.
